text.title: PM Tool Selection — Agentic Workflows and Gotchas
text.summary: Recommended subagents, prompt patterns, and failure modes for agent-driven PM tool selection.

list.item: requirements-distiller (sonnet) — distill stakeholder transcripts into MoSCoW with verbatim quote evidence.
vendor-shortlister (haiku) — apply hard constraints; return shortlist with explicit rejection reason per filtered vendor.
poc-runner (haiku, calls tool APIs) — drive scripted POC scenarios in trial instances and record latency and success.
scoring-agent (sonnet) — apply weights, compute totals, run sensitivity analysis under ±20% perturbation.
decision-writer (opus) — produce ADR with risks, mitigations, migration plan outline.
pricing-tracker (haiku, scheduled) — monitor vendor pricing pages and update comparison with date stamp.

example.code: You are requirements-distiller. Inputs: meeting_transcript.md, prior_tools.md. Emit a MoSCoW table: { "must": [...], "should": [...], "could": [...], "wont": [...] }. Each item: { "id": "R-NN", "name": "...", "evidence": [ {"quote": "...", "speaker": "...", "transcript_offset": ...} ], "metric": "...|null" }. Refuse to invent requirements without an evidence quote.

You are scoring-agent. Inputs: requirements.json, weights.json, vendor_findings/*.json. For each vendor, compute weighted score; return top 3 + sensitivity table showing ranking under ±20% weight perturbations. Highlight any item where rank flips under sensitivity.

antipattern.description: Using LLM training knowledge for current feature availability or pricing tiers.
antipattern.reason: Vendor pricing pages and tier names change quarterly. Require evidence from trial instance or dated changelog, never from LLM priors.

antipattern.description: Accepting compliance attestations from a badge or marketing page.
antipattern.reason: SOC2, ISO27001 badges without the actual audit report are unverifiable. Require signed security questionnaire (SIG Lite or CAIQ) before shortlisting.

antipattern.description: Letting an agent submit an RFP response or sign a DPA.
antipattern.reason: Procurement actions with legal consequences require human authorization. Agents produce drafts only.

rule.statement: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints are mandatory at: MoSCoW finalization, shortlist approval, POC scope, final pick, and ADR signoff.
rule.rationale: Each checkpoint represents a decision gate where errors compound exponentially downstream.

text.reference: Minimal POC scenario harness with vendor adapter pattern
